disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A 5-year history of recurrent painful deep lumps in both armpits and the groin that flare, some bursting to drain fluid, with an acute severe flare of the right armpit over the past 48 hours where several lumps have coalesced into one large, exquisitely tender swollen mass.
trigger_question_examples: Where is the swelling, when did this flare start, and have you had anything like it before?
Tell me about the lumps — how long have you been getting them and where?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've had painful lumps in both armpits and my groin on and off for about 5 years, but over the last two days my right armpit has flared up horribly — several lumps have joined into one big swollen, really tender mass.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lumps recurrently form, fill and spontaneously rupture producing thick, purulent, malodorous discharge, and previous flares have left hypertrophic scarring, raised tunnel-like tracts and paired blackhead-type spots — a chronic architecture, not a single new isolated boil.
trigger_question_examples: What happens to the lumps — do they burst, and what comes out?
Is there any old scarring, tunnel-like lumps, or blackhead-type spots in that area from before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They fill up and then burst on their own and leak thick, really smelly fluid. Over the years they've left scars, raised tunnel-like bits under the skin and some double blackhead-type spots — so it's not just one new boil, it's the same thing coming back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe, agonising, sharp/throbbing localised pain that restricts all right shoulder/arm movement and has prevented the patient dressing or sleeping — an uncontrolled acute pain crisis.
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the pain, and what does it stop you doing?
Is the pain affecting your sleep or your ability to move your arm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's agonizing — a sharp, throbbing pain. I can't lift or move my right arm at all, I couldn't sleep, and I can't even get dressed properly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No rapidly spreading, hot redness extending down the arm or across the chest wall, no red streaks — the redness and heat are confined to the swollen lump area, arguing against an established spreading cellulitis.
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness or heat spreading out down your arm or across your chest, or staying around the lumps?
Any red streaks running away from the area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — the redness and heat are just around the swollen lumps in my armpit; nothing is spreading down my arm or across my chest, and there are no red streaks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high fever and no uncontrollable shivering/rigors, and the patient does not feel systemically unwell — no features of systemic infection or sepsis (mild tachycardia is attributable to pain).
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever, or shaking chills / the shivers?
Do you feel generally unwell in yourself, or just in pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever and no shaking chills, and I don't feel unwell in myself — it's really just the pain and the swelling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The problem is multiple interconnected lumps arising in a chronic scarred/tunnelled area rather than a single, tense, isolated, newly-pointing boil — but whether any one part has become a discrete, deeply fluctuant collection that would need draining cannot be determined without in-person palpation.
trigger_question_examples: Is this one single new tense pointing boil, or several connected lumps in an area that's scarred from before?
Does any one spot feel like a tight, deep, fluid-filled pocket compared with the rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's several lumps joined together in an area that's already scarred and tunnelled from past flares, not one brand-new single boil. Honestly I can't tell if one part is a tight fluid-filled pocket — it's all just swollen and agonizing to touch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lesions arise exclusively in the flexural friction/skin-fold zones (axillae and groin) and the patient is a smoker — the mechanical friction and smoking triggers characteristic of the chronic disease.
trigger_question_examples: Where on the body do these lumps come up, and does anything seem to set them off?
Do you smoke, and do the lumps come up where skin rubs together?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They only ever come up in my armpits and groin, where the skin rubs, and rubbing and sweating seem to bring them on. And yes, I smoke.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic recurrent disease managed only with intermittent topical antibiotic cream (no current systemic therapy), with substantial psychosocial distress and reduced quality of life from the chronic pain and malodorous discharge.
trigger_question_examples: What have you been using for these so far, and how is all this affecting you day to day?
How are you coping with the recurring pain and the discharge?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I just use an antibiotic cream on and off when they flare, nothing regular. Honestly it's worn me right down — the constant pain and the smell have really hit my confidence and my quality of life.
